summary_direction: Position Paul as SRE-adjacent: production support, Linux, monitoring/readiness, incident coordination, runbooks, and financial market data stability; avoid overstating deep platform engineering.
bullets_to_emphasize: Supported Linux-based enterprise financial applications requiring production readiness and operational validation.
Coordinated incident troubleshooting, health checks, escalation, and release validation across technical teams.
Supported market data / pricing systems where timeliness, data quality, and availability were business critical.
bullets_to_rewrite_or_add: Rewrite summary to say SRE-adjacent production support, not pure SRE.
Add one bullet connecting Linux, health checks, incident coordination, and market data stability.
Avoid implying direct ownership of Kubernetes/OpenShift platform engineering.
claims_to_avoid: Do not overstate hands-on Python experience; present as learning/ramp-up unless project evidence is included.
Do not claim direct ownership of tools/platforms not actually used.
Do not position as a deep infrastructure/SRE engineer; use SRE-adjacent production support framing.
cover_letter_angle: Open with production support and market data stability, framing SRE fit as operational readiness, Linux support, monitoring awareness, and incident coordination for Pico.
interview_story_focus: Linux/application operations story: supporting production systems, checking health, and coordinating incidents.
Market data stability story: timeliness, data quality, and escalation discipline.
next_action: Use this tailoring guidance to update the resume for Pico — Site Reliability Engineer.
